news.title: Marvell Stock Was Just Added to S&P 500. Here’s Who Else Was Included and Bumped.
news.summary: Marvell Technology  and  Flex  will be added to the  index, replacing  Pool  and  Campbell’s  the soup company, prior to the opening of trading on June 22, according to S&P Dow Jones Indices. Marvell was famously tagged as the next $1 trillion company by  Nvidia  CEO Jensen Huang earlier this week.

news.title: Carnage in Chip Stocks Hits Extra Hard in Top-Heavy Market
news.summary: Shares of the chip-making giants that have powered the market’s climb to records tumbled, weighed down by new concerns that trillions of dollars invested in AI technology won’t yield the expected blockbuster returns.  The losses intensified after a robust jobs report raised new worries that the Federal Reserve may need to raise interest rates later this year to fight inflation.  Stocks, bonds, oil, gold and bitcoin all tumbled.

news.title: Market Talk: Nasdaq's slide reflects rate-hike, AI cost jitters
news.summary:
STORY: :: Lisa Bernhard, Reuters

:: Eric Lynch, Managing Director, Co-portfolio manager, Suncoast Equity Management

A blockbuster May jobs report from the U.S. Labor Department increased investors' bets that the Federal Reserve would raise interest rates before the end of the year.

Speaking with Lisa Bernhard, Lynch noted that "rumbling from corporate execs" about AI token costs and usage have led companies to start to "think more mindfully about how they spend their AI budgets."

Lynch also said that bitcoin's plunge was due to investors being drawn away from the cryptocurrency by rallies in gold and AI-linked stocks.

news.title: Chip sell-off rattles markets

news.title: Bitcoin Dive Will Hike Strategy's Borrowing Rate To 11.75% — At Least
news.summary: Strategy's sale of $2.5 million bitcoin, which helped precipitate this week's dive for the cryptocurrency, appears certain to hike its annual financing costs by $26 million.  After sinking in heavy volume to start the month, Strategy's Stretch variable rate preferred stock with the STRC ticker has an impossibly steep mountain to climb to avoid a ratcheting up of its dividend rate by at least 25 basis points to 11.75% on all $10.5 billion of the outstanding securities.  Friday's even deeper selloff has put a potential 50-basis-point hike key Strategy's borrowing rate to 13% in play.

news.title: Tech sell-off, rate-hike fears drive Wall Street plunge
news.summary: Wall Street's key indices closed heavily in the red on Friday, hit by a massive sell-off in technology stocks following a recent surge driven by AI investment, and fears of US Fed rate hikes on the horizon."However, if policymakers even start talking about rate hikes or taking a more hawkish posture, that could throw cold water on the recent stock market surge," Kenwell said.
